preview.title: Set up your Bitcoin wallet server
preview.content: Before you create the first admin password, review what this SwissKnife instance will control: wallet access, Lightning payments, on-chain deposits, reusable identity and node connectivity.
preview.empty_state: Back up your node and wallet material before sending meaningful funds.

welcome.title: One place to send and receive
welcome.content: SwissKnife gives the first admin a dashboard for Lightning, on-chain Bitcoin and reusable receive identities without splitting them into separate apps.
welcome.point_one: Receive with Lightning invoices, Bitcoin addresses or a reusable Lightning Address.
welcome.point_two: Send by pasting an invoice, LNURL, BIP21 request, address or internal recipient.
welcome.point_three: Track amounts, status and next actions from the wallet dashboard.

custody.title: Self-custody means you are responsible
custody.content: This server cannot recover funds or secrets you lose. Treat setup as the moment to decide who can administer the wallet and where backups live.
custody.point_one: Create the first local admin password before the wallet opens.
custody.point_two: Back up node and wallet recovery material before meaningful deposits.
custody.point_three: Keep the admin password and recovery material separate.

accessibility.title: Works for home, desktop and server installs
accessibility.content: This first-run flow is for self-hosted boxes, desktop runs and managed server deployments. The dashboard adapts after authentication is configured.
accessibility.point_one: Use local JWT setup for single-admin self-hosted or desktop installs.
accessibility.point_two: Use external auth when a server deployment manages accounts and roles elsewhere.
accessibility.point_three: Use regtest or test funds first when validating a new deployment.

multi_user.title: Start with one admin, grow into accounts
multi_user.content: JWT installs create one initial admin today. Server deployments with external authentication can map dashboard access to provider-side users, roles and scopes.
multi_user.point_one: Manage wallets and reusable identities from the admin dashboard.
multi_user.point_two: Create API keys for services that need wallet access.
multi_user.point_three: Add accounts, roles and agents as your deployment model requires.
